title: Configurable model + cloud-inference egress gating + audit verdict
description: Make the synthesis/critic model+provider configurable and record the critic outcome in the audit log, while keeping cloud egress strictly behind the existing cloud-inference axis. (1) Extend MedicalSectionSchema (src/config/schema.ts:374-385) with an optional `inference` block: { provider?: string; endpoint?: string; model?: string } (zod, all optional). (2) Add a resolver buildMedicalInferenceClient(config, egress) in a small module (e.g. src/medical/inference.ts) that returns { client: LLMClient; model: string }: when config.medical.inference specifies a CLOUD provider (anything other than the local openai-compat localhost / ollama default), it is used ONLY if egress.isAllowed('cloud-inference') (src/medical/egress.ts:35) is true; otherwise the resolver FAILS CLOSED — it falls back to the local default (openai-compat http://localhost:11434/v1, llama3) so no cloud egress occurs. Absent any inference config, it returns the current local default unchanged (back-compat). (3) Wire engine.ts grounded branch to obtain its LLMClient+model from buildMedicalInferenceClient(config, egress) instead of the hardcoded createClient at engine.ts:402, passing the resolved model through synthesizeGrounded to the critic. (4) Extend AuditEntry (src/medical/types.ts:77-87) with optional `criticVerdict?: 'approve' | 'reject-abstained' | 'error-abstained'` (IDs/enums only) and append it on the grounded path: approve when the gate returned an approved answer, reject-abstained when the gate abstained after a critic reject, error-abstained when it abstained due to a thrown error. No prompt text or health values; AuditLog 0600 mode preserved.

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build) and zero typecheck errors in strict mode (npm run typecheck).
The full test suite passes with zero failures including new collocated src/medical/inference.test.ts and updated engine/audit tests (npm run test); no regression in the pre-existing suite.

Given config.medical.inference specifying a cloud provider but medical.egress.cloudInference=false (default), buildMedicalInferenceClient returns the LOCAL default client (no cloud) — asserted by inspecting the resolved provider/endpoint and by a spy that fails if a cloud client is constructed/called.

Given config.medical.inference specifying a cloud provider AND medical.egress.cloudInference=true, buildMedicalInferenceClient returns a client/model built from that cloud config (proving the 'any model' capability is reachable behind the opt-in) — asserted via the resolved provider/model and an injected factory spy.

With NO config.medical.inference set, buildMedicalInferenceClient returns the exact current local default (openai-compat http://localhost:11434/v1, model llama3); a back-compat test asserts the grounded path behaves identically to Sprint 2.

After a grounded turn, the audit-<date>.jsonl entry carries exactly one criticVerdict value in {approve, reject-abstained, error-abstained} matching the gate outcome, alongside the existing event:'answer'|'abstain'; the line contains NO substring of the prompt or answer body and NO health value.
The audit file remains mode 0600 after appending the criticVerdict (stat assertion), and AuditEntry stays IDs/enums-only — criticVerdict is one of the three enum literals, never free text.

Zero outbound egress unless cloud-inference is enabled: with cloudInference=false and a network spy that throws on use, the grounded turn completes without invoking it; the scoped medical ESLint boundary still passes (npm run lint) and no new disallowed network import is added outside the providers factory seam.

nonGoals: Do not add a new egress axis — reuse cloud-inference (src/medical/egress.ts:5,35).
Do not change synthesizeGrounded's gate logic or the one-re-synthesis bound (Sprint 2).
Do not add cloud fallback chains, streaming, or caching.
Do not log prompt text, answer body, citations, or any health value into the audit entry — criticVerdict is an enum only.
Do not change the MedicalSopEngine.run signature or the zero-arg constructor; resolver is built from the config already passed to run().
stopConditions: Synthesis/critic model is configurable with cloud strictly gated by cloud-inference (fail-closed to local), back-compat is byte-identical with no config, the criticVerdict enum is audited IDs/enums-only at 0600, and build/typecheck/test/lint are green with no regression
definitionOfDone: src/config/schema.ts MedicalSectionSchema gains an optional inference:{provider?,endpoint?,model?} block. src/medical/inference.ts exports buildMedicalInferenceClient(config, egress):{client,model} — cloud only when cloudInference=true else fail-closed local default; identical local default when unset. src/medical/engine.ts grounded branch resolves its client+model via buildMedicalInferenceClient and threads the model into synthesizeGrounded. src/medical/types.ts AuditEntry gains optional criticVerdict enum; engine appends it on the grounded path. Collocated tests cover cloud-gated-off (local fallback), cloud-gated-on (cloud used), no-config back-compat, audit verdict value + PHI-free + 0600, and zero-egress-when-disabled. Build, typecheck, test, lint pass with no regression.
assumptions: EgressGuard already exposes isAllowed('cloud-inference') and is built from config via EgressGuard.fromConfig(config) (src/medical/egress.ts:25-48); the engine builds it at engine.ts:384 and can pass it to the resolver.

The providers factory createClient(provider, endpoint, apiKey?, model?) (src/providers/factory.ts, used at engine.ts:402) is the single client-construction seam; buildMedicalInferenceClient uses it and is the only place that decides local-vs-cloud.
'Local default' = provider 'openai-compat', endpoint 'http://localhost:11434/v1', model 'llama3' (engine.ts:402) — treated as non-egressing; any other provider/endpoint is treated as cloud and gated.
AuditEntry criticVerdict is optional and additive; existing entries without it remain valid (the field is only populated on the grounded answer/abstain path).

The criticVerdict value is derived from the gate result the engine already has: approved answer -> 'approve'; abstained-after-reject -> 'reject-abstained'; abstained-after-throw -> 'error-abstained'. Sprint 2's synthesizeGrounded must surface enough signal (return the verdict alongside the answer, or an enum) for the engine to classify — extend its return type if needed without changing its gate behavior.
outOfScope: Any change to upstream gates or numerics
generatorNotes:
Read FIRST: src/config/schema.ts:374-421 (MedicalSectionSchema + egress block; add the inference block as a sibling of egress, all-optional zod); src/medical/egress.ts (EgressGuard.fromConfig at :25, isAllowed at :35); src/providers/factory.ts (createClient signature + how validateApiKey behaves for cloud providers — used at fleet index.js:26); src/medical/engine.ts:383-428 (where egress is built at :384 and the client is constructed at :402, and where auditLog.append fires at :417); src/medical/retrieval/literature.ts synthesizeGrounded (Sprint 2 — you may need to widen its return to carry the verdict classification); src/medical/types.ts:64-87 (AuditEvent + AuditEntry — add the optional criticVerdict field).

schema.ts: add to MedicalSectionSchema `inference: z.object({ provider: z.string().optional(), endpoint: z.string().optional(), model: z.string().optional() }).optional()`. Keep egress untouched.

src/medical/inference.ts (new): export function buildMedicalInferenceClient(config: BoberConfig, egress: EgressGuard): { client: LLMClient; model: string }. Logic: const inf = config.medical?.inference; const LOCAL = { provider:'openai-compat', endpoint:'http://localhost:11434/v1', model:'llama3' }; const wantProvider = inf?.provider ?? LOCAL.provider; const isLocal = wantProvider === 'openai-compat' && (inf?.endpoint ?? LOCAL.endpoint).includes('localhost'); if (!isLocal && !egress.isAllowed('cloud-inference')) { /* FAIL-CLOSED */ return { client: createClient(LOCAL.provider, LOCAL.endpoint, undefined, LOCAL.model), model: LOCAL.model }; } const provider = inf?.provider ?? LOCAL.provider; const endpoint = inf?.endpoint ?? LOCAL.endpoint; const model = inf?.model ?? LOCAL.model; return { client: createClient(provider, endpoint, undefined, model), model }; Add a tiny seam (e.g. an optional injected factory param defaulting to createClient) so tests can spy without real network. Unicode section headers; import type for LLMClient/BoberConfig/EgressGuard.

engine.ts: replace the hardcoded `const llmClient = this.deps?.llmClient ?? createClient('openai-compat', ...)` at :402 with `const { client: llmClient, model: synthModel } = this.deps?.llmClient ? { client: this.deps.llmClient, model: 'llama3' } : buildMedicalInferenceClient(config, egress);` (keep deps.llmClient injection winning for tests). Thread synthModel into synthesizeGrounded so the critic uses the same model. At the audit append (:417) add criticVerdict derived from the gate outcome — extend synthesizeGrounded (Sprint 2) to return e.g. { answer, verdict: 'approve'|'reject-abstained'|'error-abstained' } and pass verdict into auditLog.append alongside event.

types.ts: add `criticVerdict?: 'approve' | 'reject-abstained' | 'error-abstained';` to AuditEntry with a doc comment 'IDs/enums only — never text'.

Tests: inference.test.ts (cloud-off->local fallback; cloud-on->cloud client via factory spy; no-config->exact local default; assert the factory spy is NEVER called with a cloud provider when cloudInference=false). engine.test.ts / audit additions: run a grounded turn and read the jsonl, assert criticVerdict is the right enum, assert the line has no prompt/answer substring, stat 0600. Keep all Sprint-2 zero-LLM and gate tests green.
evaluatorNotes: sc-3-3: config.medical.inference.provider='anthropic' (or any non-local) with egress cloudInference=false -> assert buildMedicalInferenceClient resolves provider 'openai-compat' + localhost endpoint, and the injected factory spy is never called with a cloud provider. sc-3-4: same inference but cloudInference=true -> assert the factory spy IS called with provider 'anthropic' and the configured model, and the resolved model is threaded. sc-3-5: no inference block -> assert resolved {provider:'openai-compat', endpoint includes localhost, model:'llama3'} and a grounded turn matches Sprint-2 behavior. sc-3-6/sc-3-7: run grounded approve / reject->reject / throw turns; for each read audit-<date>.jsonl, assert exactly one criticVerdict in {approve,reject-abstained,error-abstained} matching the outcome, assert NO prompt or answer substring and no numeric health value in the line, and fs.stat the file mode === 0o600. sc-3-8: with cloudInference=false and a node-level network spy (e.g. patched fetch/createClient) that throws on cloud use, assert the grounded turn completes; npm run lint clean; grep the diff for disallowed network imports outside providers/. Build+typecheck clean; FULL suite no regression (fleet + all medical).
